DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR FEES IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT SINCE THE COURT VACATED THE UNDERLYING ANTI-SLAPP ORDER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BOSCO CREDIT LLC, FRANKLIN CREDIT MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, HOMAN MOBASSER, ANGELA DAVTYAN, THOMAS AXON'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7 IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,000 AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND ATTORNEY WILLIAM E. GILG IS DENIED. EVEN THOUGH THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WAS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER, DEFENDANTS FAIL TO SHOW THAT THE MOTION WAS "FRIVOLOUS" WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7 OR THAT PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO SUBSTANTIVE RELIEF. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

ORDER RE: MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF COURT ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS' ANTI-SLAPP MOTION ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2020

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS MEHENDRA PRASAD AND GEET PRASAD'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF COURT ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS ANTI SLAPP MOTION ON SEPTEMBER 17 2020. THE COURT TREATS PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AS A MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473 AND GRANTS THE MOTION.(SEE AUSTIN V. LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT (2016) 222 CAL.APP.4TH 918, 930 [TRIAL COURT HAD DISCRETION TO TREAT MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AS MOTION FOR RELIEF PER SECTION 473].) THE COURT VACATES THE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION THAT WAS ENTERED ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2020. PLAINTIFF SOUGHT RELIEF WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME AFTER SERVICE OF THE ORDER, PLAINTIFF PROVIDES A PROPOSED OPPOSITION TO THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE, AND THE DECLARATION OF COUNSEL GILG DEMONSTRATES MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, AND/OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. DEFENDANTS' FEE REQUEST IS DENIED. THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS RESET FOR HEARING ON JANUARY 5, 2021. PLAINTIFFS SHALL IMMEDIATELY FILE THEIR OPPOSITION TO THAT MOTION AND THE REPLY IS DUE IN ACCORDANCE WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1005. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Jeffrey Lewis In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs BOSCO CREDIT LLC, FRANKLIN CREDIT MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, HOMAN MOBASSER, ANGELA DAVTYAN, THOMAS AXON's Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees:

Defendants' motion for an award of attorney's fees and costs is granted in the amount of $22,340 in fees and $2,631.56 in costs, for a total of $24,971.56. As the prevailing parties on the special motion to strike, defendants are entitled to recover their attorney's fees and costs. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 425.16(c)(1) ["a prevailing defendant on a special motion to strike shall be entitled to recover his or her attorney's fees and costs"]; Ketchum v. Moses (2001) 24 Cal.4th 1122, 1131 [under anti-SLAPP statute, "any SLAPP defendant who brings a successful motion to strike is entitled to mandatory attorney fees"]. Such fees are to be awarded utilizing the lodestar adjustment method. (Ketchum, 24 Cal.4th at 1136.) Under that approach, the court is first to set the lodestar figure reflecting the product of the number of hours reasonably expended multiplied by the reasonable hourly rate. (Id. at 1134.) It may, but is not required to, award a fee enhancement for the purpose of compensating the attorney who agreed to undertake the representation at the risk of nonpayment or delayed payment, in an amount approaching the market rate for comparable legal services. (Id. at 1136.) The trial court's application of the lodestar method to the circumstances of a specific case is reviewed under the abuse of discretion standard. (Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 491.)

Plaintiff's late-filed opposition to the motion on the ground that Defendants' counsel should have met and conferred before filing the motion is meritless. Plaintiff should not have filed the complaint in the first place; the anti-SLAPP statute contains no such requirement; and it requires that a special motion to strike be filed within 60 days of service of the complaint. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 425.16(f).) Further, if plaintiff believed that the special motion to strike was meritorious, he had ample opportunity to limit his exposure to attorney's fees by voluntarily dismissing the complaint; instead, he filed a groundless opposition. The Court will not entertain Plaintiff's attempts to relitigate the issues raised and decided in connection with that motion or with the underlying litigation. The Court declines Plaintiff's request to take judicial notice of the judgment and orders entered in the underlying litigation, which have no bearing on the resolution of the instant motion.

The Court also rejects Plaintiff's argument that Defendants have engaged in bad faith "padding" of their bills or billed for time not spent directly in connection with the special motion to strike, and finds that the amount of time for which Defendants seek compensation is reasonable. Plaintiff's own procedural failings caused Defendants' counsel to expend considerably more time than otherwise would have been required. Likewise, although Plaintiff objects to Defendants' lead counsel's hourly rate as excessive, he supplies no contrary evidence, and the Court finds based on its own knowledge of the San Francisco legal market and its assessment of counsel's expertise and work that the rate is reasonable. The Court does not believe that application of a multiplier is warranted.

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 In The Amount Of $4,000 Against Plaintiffs And Attorney William E. Gilg; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Jeffrey Lewis In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs BOSCO CREDIT LLC, FRANKLIN CREDIT MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, HOMAN MOBASSER, ANGELA DAVTYAN, THOMAS AXON's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 In The Amount Of $4,000 Against Plaintiffs And Attorney William E. Gilg:

Defendants' motion for sanctions is denied. Even though the motion for reconsideration was procedurally improper, defendants fail to show that the motion was "frivolous" within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 or that plaintiffs are not entitled to substantive relief.

Order signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Court Order Granting Defendants Anti Slapp Motion On September 17 2020
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs MEHENDRA PRASAD and GEET PRASAD's Motion For Reconsideration Of Court Order Granting Defendants Anti Slapp Motion On September 17 2020

The Court treats plaintiffs' motion for reconsideration as a motion for relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473 and grants the motion. (See Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School District (2016) 222 Cal.App.4th 918, 930 [trial court had discretion to treat motion for reconsideration as motion for relief per section 473].) The Court vacates the order granting defendant's anti-SLAPP motion that was entered on September 17, 2020. Plaintiff sought relief within a reasonable time after service of the order, plaintiff provides a proposed opposition to the anti-SLAPP motion to strike, and the declaration of counsel Gilg demonstrates mistake, inadvertence, surprise, and/or excusable neglect. Defendants' fee request is denied. The anti-SLAPP motion is reset for hearing on January 5, 2021. Plaintiffs shall immediately file their opposition to that motion and the reply is due in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure section 1005.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 In The Amount Of $4,000 Against Plaintiffs And Attorney William E. Gilg; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Jeffrey Lewis In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-12-2020 Continued To Dec-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Court Order Granting Defendants Anti Slapp Motion On September 17 2020
Off Calendar Nov-12-2020 Continued To Dec-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS MEHENDRA PRASAD AND GEET PRASAD's Motion For Reconsideration Of Court Order Granting Defendants Anti-SLAPP Motion On September 17 2020.

Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration is continued to December 9, 2020. The court agrees with defendants that the order granting the anti-SLAPP motion is a "judgment" and plaintiff can no longer move for reconsideration. (See Marshall v. Webster (2020) 54 Cal.App.5th 275, 281.) Nevertheless, plaintiff's motion was filed within a reasonable time after service of the order granting the anti-SLAPP motion as unopposed, plaintiff provides a proposed opposition to the anti-SLAPP motion to strike, and the declaration of counsel Gilg demonstrates mistake, inadvertence, surprise, and/or excusable neglect. Given this record, defendants should show cause why this court should not treat plaintiff's motion for reconsideration as a motion for relief per CCP 473(b) and vacate the court's prior order. (See Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School District (2016) 222 Cal.App.4th 918, 930 [trial court had discretion to treat motion for reconsideration as motion for relief per CCP 473].)

Defendants may file a supplemental brief by November 24, 2020 addressing the propriety of granting relief per CCP 473 and whether fees are appropriate. Plaintiff may respond to defendants' filing by December 2, 2020

Defendant's motion for sanctions is continued from December 4, 2020 to December 9, 2020.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
